prologue_speak_to_elder.title: Listen to the Land
prologue_speak_to_elder.narrative_goal: Learn how village life is sustained by land, work, and community.
prologue_speak_to_elder.historical_context: Kenyan societies had diverse systems of farming, grazing, trade, and local governance before colonial rule.
prologue_speak_to_elder.journal_entry: Village Elder: Before new borders and railways, communities governed land, trade, and family life through their own traditions.

prologue_meet_the_village.title: Voices of the Village
prologue_meet_the_village.narrative_goal: Listen to the people whose work, knowledge, and care sustain the community.
prologue_meet_the_village.historical_context: Pre-colonial Kenyan societies were diverse, with community life shaped by farming, craft, exchange, and shared responsibilities.
prologue_meet_the_village.journal_entry: Community Resilience: Daily life depended on many connected forms of work, knowledge, exchange, and mutual care.

prologue_collect_memories.title: Traces of Everyday Life
prologue_collect_memories.narrative_goal: Find objects that reveal how ordinary skills and exchange connect the village.
prologue_collect_memories.historical_context: Material culture, such as tools, containers, and trade goods, can reveal how people worked, travelled, and exchanged knowledge.
prologue_collect_memories.journal_entry: Everyday Objects: Tools and crafted objects can hold stories about work, exchange, skill, and community life.
